summary: Add clean skeleton, manifest-based init/upgrade, harness checks, and README onboarding.
next_action: Open a PR for codex/harness-distribution-hardening, then merge after review.
acceptance_criteria: A manifest classifies harness-owned, project-owned, managed, and excluded files.
A clean skeleton initializes project-owned planning files without live harness development history.
Init installs into a target that is not this git repository and refuses partial overwrites.
Upgrade preserves project-owned state and reports conflicts for locally modified harness files.
Check validates JSON, command/mode consistency, clean skeleton contamination, stale phase references, installed targets, and optional changed-path enforcement.
README documents init, upgrade, check, file ownership, and PR-before-merge verification.
verification: tmp="$(mktemp -d)"; python3 scripts/harness.py init --target "$tmp/target"; python3 scripts/harness.py check --target "$tmp/target"; (cd "$tmp/target" && python3 scripts/harness.py check)
notes: Upgrade is run from a newer harness source tree against a target project.
Installed targets can run local check without a source manifest.
